worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: The breakout post on Anthropic's pricing blunder — spotted the contradiction that $20/mo Cowork is just Claude Code with a friendlier name, making the removal self-defeating.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Best single-post resolution of the pricing saga: Anthropic reverted silently, Simon's writeup is the clearest account of what actually happened and what's still unresolved.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Solid numbers breakdown on the SpaceX/Cursor $60B option deal — puts the valuation in context against Cursor's $9.9B from last year and what xAI is actually buying.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Sharp read on OpenAI's rate-limit reset announcement as a deliberate poaching move targeting frustrated Claude Code power users — directly relevant to competitive positioning.

worthClicking.author: @levie
worthClicking.why: Aaron Levie on why enterprise agent adoption requires heavy implementation help — the 'real world has legacy stacks' argument is the core sales motion for anyone selling into enterprise AI.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: Crisp framing: Anthropic employee #200 is getting outcomes that historically required being employee #20 at Google — a real distribution/leverage argument for indie builders.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: The best product strategy take in the feed: you don't beat a protocol by building a better client, you make the client irrelevant — directly applicable to distribution thinking.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: GitHub Copilot pausing new signups entirely is a meaningful competitive signal that's easy to miss in the Claude Code pricing noise.

worthClicking.author: @jeff_weinstein
worthClicking.why: Jeff Weinstein actively recruiting devs building economic agents this week — if you're building in this space, this is a live opportunity.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Concrete math on Claude Code's cost arbitrage — 10B tokens for $200/mo vs $15K at API rates — the sanctioned cheap-at-scale angle is the actual product story Anthropic should be telling.

voices.why: Most rigorous real-time tracking of the Claude Code pricing debacle with actual receipts — his blog posts are the primary source everyone else is citing.
Consistent concrete breakdowns on Anthropic product economics, SpaceX/Cursor deal, and OpenAI competitive moves — signal-dense even if volume is high.
Sharp contrarian product takes (distribution disruption thesis, Anthropic valuation leverage) with low word count and high concept density.
Building live economic agent infrastructure at Stripe and sharing real practitioner questions about agent trust signals — directly on the operator's interests.
The enterprise agent adoption framing post had 118K views for a reason — he's articulating the sales motion reality that pure AI-hype accounts miss.

examples: newsletter self-promo spam (aakashgupta posting identical 'subscribe to my newsletter' 4+ times)
GM/good vibes filler posts with no information content
flying cars / robotics / EV factory tourism unrelated to operator interests

fading.theme: Anthropic compute scale-up (5GW AWS deal continuation)
Lovable security incident & vibe-coded app risks
model competition (Kimi K2.6 vs Claude, Codex reliability)
